packageRules.description: cargo: group minor/patch; majors stay individual
cargo: pre-1.0 crates treat 0.x→0.y as major (Cargo semver convention); ungroup from minor/patch so they land as individual PRs
dockerfile: group minor/patch; majors stay individual

Pinned git-rev cargo deps: inkwell/melior are forks tied to the LLVM 21.1 backend, slang_solidity is pinned for __private_backend_api compat, web3 (tomusdrw) is archived upstream. Disable Renovate's default git-digest updates — these are bumped manually when feature/build compat warrants. Note: matches on depName (Cargo.toml key), not packageName (which is the git URL for git-source deps).

Pin reqwest to 0.12.x until web3 (tomusdrw, transitive) also moves off 0.12. solx-compiler-downloader uses reqwest with default-features = false and only ["blocking", "json"] — no TLS feature. While web3's reqwest 0.12 lock entry is the same major, cargo feature unification adds web3's TLS features (http-rustls-tls) to the shared compiled crate and HTTPS works for free. Splitting majors (0.12 here, 0.13 there) breaks unification, leaves the 0.13 build with no TLS, and HTTPS fails with `invalid URL, scheme is not http`. PR #399 hit exactly this and PR #406's URL switch couldn't fix it. `allowedVersions` keeps 0.12.x patch bumps (security fixes) flowing while blocking the 0.13 jump. Drop this rule when rust-web3 itself moves to reqwest 0.13.
solx-ci-runner is a private GHCR package
